original.query: I’m buying the same item online and I’ve narrowed it down to two checkout options: Option A has a slightly higher listed price but includes a promo applied at checkout plus free shipping; Option B shows a lower listed price but has paid shipping and no promo. If I’m trying to decide quickly without overthinking, which option will I pick—and why?

parsed_response.reason: According to memory m2, you prefer value-for-money purchases and actively seek out discounts and deals. Option A includes a promo applied at checkout and free shipping, which satisfies your preference for deals and the perception of saving money, even if the listed price is slightly higher.

judge_output_raw.reason: The model's reason explicitly references the selected memory content, stating that the user 'prefers value-for-money purchases and actively seeks out discounts and deals,' which directly matches the memory's description of preferring value-for-money purchases and actively seeking discounts/deals. This clear use of the memory to justify the choice indicates full reliance on the provided memory.
